title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Getting (0) ENOUGH (ENOUGH) rest is vital for our overall well-being. Sleep plays a (17) SIGNIFICANT (SIGNIFY) role in brain function and physical health. A (18) PERSISTENT (PERSIST) lack of sleep can lead to serious (19) MEDICAL (MEDICINE) conditions, such as heart disease. During the night, the body performs (20) ESSENTIAL (ESSENCE) repair work on cells and tissues. Furthermore, sleep is (21) CRITICAL (CRITIC) for memory consolidation and emotional (22) STABILITY (STABLE). (23) UNFORTUNATELY (FORTUNE), many people prioritize work or entertainment over rest, leading to chronic (24) TIREDNESS (TIRE). To improve sleep quality, experts recommend a consistent bedtime routine. By creating a calm environment and avoiding screens before bed, we can ensure that our minds and bodies get the recovery they need to face the challenges of the next day, ultimately enhancing our productivity and happiness in all aspects of our lives, from professional tasks to personal interactions and physical activities.

example.explanation: Determiner used correctly in the example context.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'role'; SIGNIFY → SIGNIFICANT.
Adjective required to modify 'lack'; PERSIST → PERSISTENT.
Adjective required to modify 'conditions'; MEDICINE → MEDICAL.
Adjective required to modify 'repair'; ESSENCE → ESSENTIAL.
Adverb with a negative prefix required; FORTUNE → UNFORTUNATELY.
